summary:
Claim: The lateral pterygoid muscle restricts distolingual extension in maxillary complete dentures through posterior condylar displacement during jaw opening.  
Evidence: - During mouth opening, the lateral pterygoid muscle pulls the condyles posteriorly, creating mechanical resistance to excessive distolingual extension. - This posterior displacement acts as a natural limit preventing overextension beyond physiological ranges.  
Answer: C
